title: Implement real health probes and durable event streaming
issueBody:
## Summary

Two infrastructure systems are stubs that need real implementations to support production operations.

## 1. Health Probes

The adapter health check returns `{ status: 'unknown', reason: 'not-implemented' }`. The health page shows Connected/Error based on env var presence + simple fetch, but doesn't do deep probes.

**What's needed:**
- TCP/HTTP probe to Gitea (`KRATE_GITEA_HTTP_URL/api/v1/version`)
- HTTP probe to Agent Mux Gateway (`AGENT_MUX_URL/healthz`)
- HTTP probe to Krate Controller API (`KRATE_CONTROLLER_URL/healthz`)
- kubectl connectivity check (`kubectl cluster-info`)
- Anthropic API key validation (test with a minimal API call or key format check)

**Files:** `agent-adapter-controller.js`, `agent-stack-controller.js`, health API route

## 2. Durable Event Streaming

The SSE endpoint emits events via an in-process event bus. Issues:
- Events don't survive server restarts (JSONL ring buffer persists 1000 events but isn't replicated)
- Multiple server replicas each have their own event bus — no cross-replica sync
- No backpressure or guaranteed delivery

**What's needed:**
- Replace in-memory event bus with Redis pub/sub, NATS, or similar
- SSE endpoint subscribes to durable topic instead of local bus
- Event persistence to queryable store (not just JSONL append)
- The Helm chart already includes optional NATS (`demo.nats`) — wire it up

**Files:** `event-bus.js`, `app/api/orgs/[org]/agents/events/stream/route.js`

## Reference

- [docs/gaps/staging-status.md](packages/krate/docs/gaps/staging-status.md) — health section
- [docs/gaps/infrastructure-deps.md](packages/krate/docs/gaps/infrastructure-deps.md) — service dependencies

## Dependencies

- #608 (staging env vars) — probes need services to probe
issueCommentsSummary: Triage confirmed #612 is valid production-readiness work. Existing staging has partial health/SSE behavior: adapter and MCP endpoint checks exist when a resource endpoint is configured, web snapshot probes Kubernetes by listing Organization, Gitea at /api/v1/version, and Agent Mux/Gateway at /health, while event-bus.js still uses a process-local Set and local JSONL ring buffer. Recommended implementation is two tracks: shared bounded health probes for Gitea, Agent Mux /healthz, Krate Controller /healthz, Kubernetes connectivity, and assistant credential validation; and a durable event transport abstraction with in-memory fallback plus NATS/JetStream production transport, replay cursors, retention, backpressure, reconnect behavior, and broker failure reporting. Previous planning PR #694 used branch plan/issue-612 and was closed after the issue was closed. Comment also references implementation PR #702 with health probes, memory + NATS/JetStream event transport, Helm values/templates, docs, and tests; the process must verify whether that work is present on the current branch before reimplementing.
acceptanceCriteria: Health API/snapshot output includes deep structured probes for Gitea at KRATE_GITEA_HTTP_URL/api/v1/version, Agent Mux/Gateway at AGENT_MUX_URL or AGENT_GATEWAY_URL /healthz, Krate Controller at KRATE_CONTROLLER_URL/healthz, Kubernetes connectivity, and assistant credentials.
Health probes run concurrently with strict timeouts, partial failure reporting, injectable dependencies for tests, and no secret leakage.
Agent adapter and stack health behavior remains backward compatible while removing stale not-implemented semantics where real endpoints exist.
Production event streaming uses NATS/JetStream or an equivalent durable broker when configured and no longer depends on process-local-only fanout for multi-replica deployments.

The event transport preserves createEventBus/globalEventBus compatibility for local tests while adding stable event IDs, replay cursor support, cross-subscriber fanout, retention semantics, backpressure behavior, reconnect handling, and broker outage status.
Core HTTP SSE and Next.js agents events stream route subscribe through the durable transport abstraction and support replay from Last-Event-ID or an explicit cursor where practical.
Helm values/templates wire optional NATS/event transport configuration into the relevant workloads without embedding secrets or breaking local default installs.
Docs and gaps references describe the new operational behavior and remaining staging validation dependencies.
nonGoals: Do not implement unrelated API route fixes, UI redesigns, or broad Krate controller refactors.
Do not commit real Anthropic, Gitea, NATS, Kubernetes, or staging credentials.
Do not require live external services for unit tests; use injectable clients/runners and reserve live checks for explicit staging smoke validation.
Do not remove the in-memory event bus path needed for local development and deterministic tests.
expectedRuntimeCallPaths: Web HealthMonitor -> GET /api/orgs/{org}/snapshot -> packages/krate/web/app/api/orgs/[org]/snapshot/route.js -> shared health probe service -> Kubernetes/Gitea/Agent Mux/Krate Controller/assistant probes
Krate API controller resource mutations -> packages/krate/core/src/api-controller.js -> globalEventBus.emitResourceChange -> event transport abstraction -> memory or NATS/JetStream publish/persist
Core controller SSE -> packages/krate/core/src/http-server.js GET /api/orgs/{org}/agents/events/stream -> event transport subscription/replay -> text/event-stream
Web SSE -> packages/krate/web/app/api/orgs/[org]/agents/events/stream/route.js -> controller stream proxy when configured, otherwise event transport subscription/replay -> text/event-stream
Helm chart values -> packages/krate/charts/templates/deployments.yaml -> KRATE_* and NATS/event transport env vars for API, controllers, and web workloads
verificationCommands: node --test tests/deployment.test.js --test-name-pattern='auth chart|staging service|nats|event' (from packages/krate/core if required by the repo test layout)
